data.title: The U.S. PPI in February recorded the smallest increase since July 2024, and the U.S. dollar index fell by about 15 points in the short term.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The monthly rate of PPI in the United States in February was 0%, the smallest increase since July 2024. After the release of PPI data, the US dollar index DXY fell by about 15 points in the short term to 103.69. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. February PPI Records Minimal Increase Since July 2024
data.description: According to Odaily, the Producer Price Index (PPI) in the United States for February recorded a 0% month-on-month change, marking the smallest increase since July 2024. Following the release of the PPI data, the U.S. Dollar Index (DXY) experienced a brief decline of approximately 15 points, settling at 103.69.

data.title: U.S. Weekly Jobless Claims Slightly Lower Than Expected
data.description: According to Odaily, the number of initial jobless claims in the United States for the week ending March 8 was reported at 220,000. This figure is slightly below the anticipated 225,000 claims. The previous week's data was revised from 221,000 to 222,000.

data.title: U.S. short-term interest rate futures point to a June rate cut by the Fed after the release of U.S. initial claims and PPI data
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: After the release of U.S. initial claims and PPI data, U.S. short-term interest rate futures still show that the Federal Reserve will cut interest rates in June. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitfinex analyst: Bitcoin ETF fund flows have shown early signs of market recovery
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Bitfinex analysts pointed out that adjustments within the bull market cycle are typical and may indicate a temporary shock rather than the beginning of a long bear market. "The broader market, especially stock and bond yields, will determine the trend of Bitcoin. If global liquidity conditions ease and institutional fund inflows continue, Bitcoin's support at $72,000-75,000 may be maintained, which may lay the foundation for resuming the upward trend. 
Analysts said: "Bitcoin ETF fund flows have shown early signs of recovery, with net inflows on March 12, breaking five consecutive days of outflows, and ARK Invest's ETF fund inflows are strong, indicating that despite short-term fluctuations, institutional demand remains intact. If fund inflows continue, this may indicate that fund managers are re-adjusting their positions for the Fed's policy shift to dovishness." (Theblock)

data.title: Hyperliquid’s 50x leveraged whale has made a profit of $20,000 on its ETH long orders on GMX
BNB Surpasses 580 USDT with a 3.87% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 13, 2025, 12:24 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 580 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 580.469971 USDT, with a narrowed 3.87% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Mezo launches mUSD stablecoin collateralized by Bitcoin
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, Bitcoin expansion network Mezo announced the launch of mUSD, a stablecoin collateralized by Bitcoin. Currently, mUSD is available on the test network and the waiting list is now open.

data.title: Robinhood to List PENGU, PNUT and Popcat
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, Robinhood US announced that it will launch Pudgy Penguins (PENGU), Peanut the Squirrel (PNUT) and Popcat.

data.title: Economic Uncertainty Challenges Fed's Rate Decisions
data.description: According to Odaily, Generali Investments analyst Paolo Zanghieri highlighted in a report that the unpredictable economic policy statements from U.S. President Donald Trump's administration have introduced significant uncertainty, complicating the Federal Reserve's tasks. Zanghieri noted that the health of the U.S. economy suggests the Federal Reserve is unlikely to rush into rate cuts until some policy uncertainties are resolved. He referred to comments made by Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell last week, stating, "We expect this to be the main message of the March meeting, with the Federal Open Market Committee's current stance—two more rate cuts this year and two more in 2026—remaining unchanged."
